What We’re Looking For:



Detail-oriented and quality-focused

Fast, efficient, and reliable

Someone who takes pride in delivering clean, professional work every time

title: Creative Director
description:
Full-Time | 42 hours for 6 days a week | $1500 up to $2000



We’re looking for someone who is chronically online in the best way possible.



The type of person who:



• Saves TikToks and Reels constantly

• Knows when a video is about to go viral

• Understands trends before they become trends

• Thinks about content ideas while scrolling



If social media feels less like work and more like second nature, keep reading.



This role is for someone who wants to turn their love for content into a real career.



You’ll be helping create high-performing short-form videos and visuals designed to reach millions of viewers across social media platforms.



This isn’t just an editing job. You’ll also help shape the creative direction of content — spotting trends, improving ideas, and helping posts perform better.



What You’ll Be Doing:



- You’ll work with our social media team to create engaging short-form content for platforms like Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts.

- Your responsibilities will include editing videos, working with images, improving visuals, and helping guide the creative direction of posts so they perform better online.

- You’ll also help identify trends, analyze content performance, and contribute ideas that help our social media pages grow.



This Role Is Perfect For Someone Who:



• Is always on social media and understands internet culture

• Loves creating or editing short-form videos

• Naturally notices what makes content go viral

• Has strong English communication skills

• Has a good eye for visuals, aesthetics, and storytelling

• Is reliable, hardworking, and takes responsibility for their work

• Wants to grow their skills in social media and content creation



Experience editing short-form videos is important. Experience in creative direction is a bonus, but not required.



What matters most is that you’re creative, curious, and eager to learn.



Why This Is a Great Opportunity:



• Fully remote work

• Paid training

• Long-term position with growth opportunities

• No micromanagement

• Work on content seen by large audiences

• Opportunities for promotion and salary increases based on performance



To apply, please send:

1. A 1-minute video introduction telling us about yourself and why you'd be a good fit

2. A link to your creative portfolio 



NOTE: Applications without a video introduction will not be reviewed!



We’re looking for people who bring creativity, personality, and strong work ethic — not generic applications.



If you’re passionate about social media and want to turn that passion into a career, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Full-time Social Media Manager
description:
Full-Time | Remote | Long-Term

42 hours/week | 6 days/week

$1,000 – $1,400 USD/month (based on experience)



Who This Role Is For:



This role is for someone who genuinely loves social media and understands how Instagram works today (trends, formats, timing, and why certain posts go viral)



You don’t need years of professional experience!

What we care about most is your passion for content creation and your understanding of what makes content trend and perform well.



If you’re the type who notices patterns on your feed, saves posts for “content inspo,” or can explain why a Reel popped off then this role will be a great fit.



Your responsibilities include:

- Posting content daily across multiple Instagram accounts

- Writing captions and stories that feel natural and engaging

- Monitoring post performance (reach, saves, shares, engagement)

- Making smart posting decisions based on data and patterns

- Spotting trending formats and adapting them quickly

- Reviewing content carefully before posting to avoid mistakes

- Sharing insights with the content team on what’s working and what’s not



You’ll be trained on how to analyze metrics, understand posting patterns, and improve performance over time.



What We’re Looking For:

- Strong interest in social media and content creation

- Good understanding of social media trends and viral content

- Attention to detail and strong judgment

- Comfortable working independently in a remote setup

- Willingness to learn and improve

- Experience is a plus BUT NOT REQUIRED

- Personal pages, passion projects, or content you’ve made for fun all count as experience.



Why You’ll Like This Role:

- Fully remote work

- No micromanagement

- Paid training and regular feedback

- Opportunity to work on accounts with large audiences

- Clear growth path into strategist or leadership roles

- Supportive and healthy team culture



How to Apply (Please Read Carefully)

To be considered, your application must include:

1. A portfolio (this can be client work, personal Instagram pages, Reels, TikToks, or any creative projects.)

2. A short video introduction (1–2 minutes): Tell us why you’re a great fit. Be creative &  show your personality. We are not looking for a boring or scripted intro.

3. The number “100” in your application subject line (this helps us know you read the full post)



IMPORTANT!!!!!!!

*No video introduction = no review

*Boring or low-effort video = automatic pass



We’re looking for someone creative, detail-oriented, and passionate about social media not just someone who can follow instructions.



If this sounds like you, apply. We’re excited to see what you bring!

title: YouTube Video Editor (VA) | Long-Form Documentary Style | HeyGen & CapCut Expert
description:
?? IMPORTANT: READ BEFORE APPLYING ??

Do not apply if you intend to ghost. We are looking for a dedicated partner for a 2+ year commitment. Ghosting is a waste of your time and ours. Only apply if you are serious, reliable, and capable of meeting strict daily deadlines.



Overview

We are looking for a dedicated, long-term Video Editor/VA to join our production team. You will be responsible for creating 20-minute, documentary-style YouTube videos similar in quality and pacing to the Frum Report channel.

This is a high-volume role for an editor who wants to grow with a scaling YouTube business.

The Workflow

Tools Provided: You will have full access to our HeyGen and Storyblocks accounts.

Editing Software: Proficiency in CapCut is required.

Efficiency: You are encouraged to use CapCut’s "Script to Video" AI B-roll features to streamline the assembly process alongside Storyblocks assets.

Content Style: High-quality A/B rolls, historical/contextual B-roll, and AI-integrated visuals.

Volume: 2 videos per day.

Turnaround: Strict 24-hour delivery per video.

Requirements

RELIABILITY IS NON-NEGOTIABLE: You must communicate clearly and meet every deadline.

Proven experience with HeyGen, Storyblocks, and CapCut.

Experience using AI-assisted B-roll tools (like CapCut’s script-to-video) to speed up production.

Expert-level knowledge of A/B roll transitions and pacing for long-form content.

Ability to maintain high quality in a fast-paced, high-output environment.

Availability for full-time hours.

Compensation & Growth

Starting Rate: $5 USD per 20-minute video.

Scalable Pay: Base fees will increase automatically once the channel hits monetization milestones.

Performance Bonuses: Significant bonus for every video that exceeds 100,000 views.

Stability: Full-time, consistent work with monthly payments.

How to Apply

If you are a serious professional looking for a 2-year+ stable position, please reply with:

A link to your portfolio or specific examples of long-form documentary videos you have edited.

Confirmation that you can meet the strict 24-hour deadline for 2 videos per day.

A brief explanation of your experience with HeyGen, Storyblocks, and CapCut’s AI features.

Note: If you are looking for a short-term gig or cannot commit to this volume, do not apply.

title: STR Operations & Executive Assistant (Hospitable, Quo, ClickUp, Claude/Perplexity)
description:
Short-Term Rental Operations & Executive Assistant (Hospitable, ET Hours)



About us

We are a growing short-term rental business with 4 active listings managed through Hospitable. We consistently book most weekends and are building a streamlined, systems-driven operation. I'm a busy physician/owner and am looking for a long-term partner who can help run day-to-day STR operations and also support me as a professional assistant as the business grows.



Role overview

You will start as a Short-Term Rental Operations & Executive Assistant, with a clear path to grow into an Operations Lead role as we add more properties and complexity. This is an hourly, remote, independent-contractor position with required overlap in US Eastern Time and consistent weekend coverage.



Key outcomes (first 60–90 days)

- Guest messages are answered within agreed response times during your coverage window, in a warm, professional, hospitality-focused tone.

- Hospitable inbox and calendars are clean and accurate (no double bookings, correct rules, templates kept up to date).

- Cleaners and turnovers are coordinated smoothly, with minimal surprises for guests or vendors.

- Bookkeeper receives organized statements, invoices, receipts uploaded to Google Drive weekly.

- My personal schedule and STR-related admin are more organized and predictable because of your support.



Core responsibilities



Short-term rental operations

- Manage guest communication across Airbnb/OTAs using Hospitable (pre-stay questions, check-in issues, mid-stay questions, check-out, reviews when needed).

- Follow and improve existing messaging templates; document new edge cases and solutions as SOPs.

- Monitor reservations, calendar changes, special requests, and potential issues, and escalate to me only when needed.

- Coordinate turnovers: schedule and confirm cleaners, share guest details and special notes, follow up for photos, and flag issues (damage, missing items, supply restocking).

- Track basic property issues/maintenance requests and help arrange vendors when requested.



Bookkeeper coordination & admin

- Collect and upload STR statements, invoices, receipts, payouts to organized Google Drive folders weekly.

- Coordinate with bookkeeper: flag unusual charges, provide missing documents, answer basic questions.

- Maintain organized digital records (receipts, invoices, vendor info, warranties, etc.) in Google Drive.

- Prepare simple weekly summary reports of bookings, payouts, and expenses for bookkeeper review.



Executive assistant support

- Help manage my calendar for STR-related calls, vendor meetings, and important deadlines.

- Assist with inbox triage (tagging/organizing STR-related
s, flagging items that need my attention).

- Conduct research and present concise options (e.g., cleaners, vendors, furnishing or supplies, local services).

- Support small personal/administrative tasks as agreed (e.g., simple travel research, document organization).



Tools you'll use

- Hospitable (PMS)

- Airbnb/other OTAs as needed

- Quo (business phone system for calls/texts)

- Turnover/cleaning coordination tools (or cleaner communication via WhatsApp/SMS/email)

- ClickUp (task/project management)

- Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive, Calendar)

- AI productivity tools: Claude.ai, Perplexity.ai (for research, SOP drafting, guest message optimization, vendor analysis)

- Messaging (e.g., Slack/WhatsApp)

- Time-tracking tool (e.g., Clockify/Upwork tracker) for hourly work



Requirements

- Excellent written and spoken English; you must be able to write clear, friendly, professional guest messages and internal updates.

- Prior experience with short-term rentals, hospitality, or guest communication (Airbnb / Vrbo / 
 / STR-focused VA work strongly preferred).

- Comfortable using Hospitable, Quo, ClickUp, Claude.ai, and Perplexity.ai to improve efficiency (guest calls/texts via Quo, task tracking in ClickUp, researching cleaners/vendors via Perplexity, drafting SOPs via Claude, optimizing workflows).

- Strong organizational skills for Google Drive file management and bookkeeper coordination.

- Comfortable using PMS tools (Hospitable experience is a big plus) and learning new software quickly.

- Ability to follow SOPs and also suggest improvements when you see better ways to do things.

- Reliable internet, backup connectivity plan, and a quiet environment for calls when needed.

- Consistent overlap of at least 4 hours per day with US Eastern Time, plus reliable availability during key weekend check-in/check-out windows.



Nice to have

- Direct experience with Hospitable.

- Experience coordinating cleaners/maintenance for STRs.

- Experience organizing financial documents for accountants/bookkeepers.

- Experience supporting a busy professional (EA, PA, or ops assistant role).

- Experience using Quo (business phone), ClickUp, Claude.ai, or Perplexity.ai for operations, calls/texts, task management, research, or guest communication.



Compensation & schedule

- Independent-contractor role, hourly.

- Expected range: USD 10–15 per hour, depending on experience and fit.

- Approx. 20–30 hours per week to start, with the possibility to increase as the business grows and as you take on more responsibility.

- Weekly payment (after submitted and approved timesheet) via agreed method.

- Clear performance-based review at 60 and 90 days, with potential rate increase based on reliability, ownership, and results.



How to apply (read carefully)

To be considered, please follow these steps exactly:



1. Subject line: Use "ET STR Ops & EA – [Your Name]".

2. Loom video (2–3 minutes): Introduce yourself and briefly describe:

   - Your STR/VA/EA experience.

   - A challenging guest or client situation you handled and how you resolved it.

3. Written answers (in your message):

   - Where are you located and what are your typical working hours in your local time?

   - What is your internet setup and backup plan?

   - Describe your experience with Airbnb/STR operations and any tools like Hospitable, Quo, ClickUp, Claude.ai, Perplexity.ai, etc.

   - Give an example of how you stay organized when you're juggling multiple tasks (guests, cleaners, bookkeeper coordination, and admin).

   - What experience do you have with Quo (business phone), ClickUp, Claude.ai, or Perplexity.ai? Give 1 specific example.

4. Samples (if you have them):

   - One or two anonymized examples of guest messages you've written (before/after a tricky situation), or a brief summary of a process you helped improve.



Only candidates who follow the instructions above will be considered. This is a long-term role with room to grow, and I'm looking for someone who wants to become a trusted partner in the business—not just a short-term task taker.
